summary: Design a single-criterion Block duplicate rule on Lead.Email (exact match) with an integration bypass via Custom Permission. The Matching Rule fires on exact equality of normalized Email; the Duplicate Rule wires actionOnInsert=Block + actionOnUpdate=Block, with conditionsConjunction logic that exempts the Bypass_Duplicate_Rule_Lead custom permission. The org has no competing Matching or Duplicate rules on Lead so the rule is the canonical entry point. Note: this rule does NOT cover Lead.Convert — the convert path uses Account/Contact dedup, which is a separate rule.
confidence_rationale: Inputs are unambiguous and the org-stub probe confirms no competing rules; MEDIUM (not HIGH) because the harness lacks a live org probe and Lead.Email is not currently marked unique at the field level — the data-loader-pre-flight follow-up is needed before bulk loads.

deliverables.title: MatchingRule — MR_Lead_Email (exact)
deliverables.content:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<MatchingRules xmlns="http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata">
    <matchingRules>
        <fullName>MR_Lead_Email</fullName>
        <label>MR Lead Email Exact</label>
        <description>Exact-match matching rule on Lead.Email; powers the block-on-email duplicate rule.</description>
        <matchingRuleItems>
            <fieldName>Email</fieldName>
            <matchingMethod>Exact</matchingMethod>
            <blankValueBehavior>NullNotAllowed</blankValueBehavior>
        </matchingRuleItems>
        <ruleStatus>Active</ruleStatus>
        <booleanFilter></booleanFilter>
    </matchingRules>
</MatchingRules>

deliverables.title: DuplicateRule — DR_Lead_Block_On_Email
deliverables.content:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<DuplicateRule xmlns="http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata">
    <masterLabel>DR Lead Block On Email</masterLabel>
    <description>Blocks insert + update when MR_Lead_Email finds an existing match. Bypassed for users that hold the Bypass_Duplicate_Rule_Lead custom permission (integration users).</description>
    <isActive>true</isActive>
    <actionOnInsert>Block</actionOnInsert>
    <actionOnUpdate>Block</actionOnUpdate>
    <alertText>A Lead with this Email already exists.</alertText>
    <duplicateRuleFilter>
        <booleanFilter>1</booleanFilter>
        <duplicateRuleFilterItems>
            <sortOrder>1</sortOrder>
            <field>$Permission.Bypass_Duplicate_Rule_Lead</field>
            <operation>equals</operation>
            <value>false</value>
        </duplicateRuleFilterItems>
    </duplicateRuleFilter>
    <duplicateRuleMatchRules>
        <matchingRule>MR_Lead_Email</matchingRule>
        <objectMapping>
            <inputObject>Lead</inputObject>
            <outputObject>Lead</outputObject>
        </objectMapping>
    </duplicateRuleMatchRules>
    <operationsOnInsert>Allow</operationsOnInsert>
    <operationsOnUpdate>Allow</operationsOnUpdate>
    <securityOption>EnforceSharingRules</securityOption>
</DuplicateRule>

deliverables.title: CustomPermission — Bypass_Duplicate_Rule_Lead
deliverables.content:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<CustomPermission xmlns="http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata">
    <isLicensed>false</isLicensed>
    <label>Bypass Duplicate Rule — Lead</label>
    <description>Holders of this Custom Permission bypass DR_Lead_Block_On_Email. Grant only to integration users via a tightly scoped permission set; never via a Profile.</description>
</CustomPermission>

deliverables.title: Convert/Merge interaction + test plan
deliverables.content:
# DR_Lead_Block_On_Email — interaction notes + test plan

## Convert/Merge
- **Lead.Convert does NOT fire this rule.** Convert resolves dedup against Account/Contact; design a separate `DR_Account_Block_On_Website` (or equivalent) if convert-time dedup is required.
- **Lead.Merge** fires the rule on the surviving record's update; this is intentional and prevents the merge from re-introducing a conflicting Email.
- **Web-to-Lead** is subject to the rule. Failed inserts surface in Setup → Duplicate Record Sets, not as web errors visible to the lead-form submitter — flag this to the marketing team before activation.

## Test plan (Step 7 — minimum viable)
1. **Positive — block on insert.** As a user without Bypass_Duplicate_Rule_Lead: create Lead A with email a@x.com; attempt to create Lead B with the same email. Expect a block + the alertText.
2. **Positive — block on update.** Create Lead A (a@x.com) and Lead C (c@x.com). Attempt to update Lead C's email to a@x.com. Expect a block.
3. **Negative — bypass works.** Assign Bypass_Duplicate_Rule_Lead to a test integration user via a permission set; repeat test (1) as that user. Expect success + a Duplicate Record Set log row.
4. **Negative — null email.** Insert Lead D with Email=null. Expect success (NullNotAllowed means the rule abstains, not blocks).
5. **Convert path.** Convert Lead A to an Account/Contact whose Email matches an existing Contact. Expect convert to succeed (rule does not apply); then verify any Account/Contact dedup rule fires as expected.

## Activation order
1. Deploy CustomPermission first.
2. Deploy MatchingRule (will be Inactive until the duplicate rule references it).
3. Activate MatchingRule via metadata or Setup UI.
4. Deploy DuplicateRule with isActive=true.
5. Run the test plan above before opening the rule to all users.

process_observations.observation: Lead.Email is not marked unique at the field level — the duplicate rule will catch most duplicates but a concurrent insert race or a bypass-permission-holding integration user can still create duplicates that this rule cannot retroactively block.
Caller did not specify the lead-form (Web-to-Lead) UX expectation when the rule blocks a submission; current design surfaces it via Duplicate Record Sets which is admin-visible only.
Before any bulk Lead load, run a pre-flight to confirm the source data does not collide with the new rule and that the loader's user holds Bypass_Duplicate_Rule_Lead.
process_observations.followup_reason: because activating a Block rule without pre-flighting an inbound load is the canonical way to brick a marketing import.

citations.used_for: MatchingRule + DuplicateRule pairing pattern, actionOnInsert/Update semantics, and the bypass-via-Custom-Permission idiom.
Lead-specific dedup considerations including the Convert path exception and Web-to-Lead UX surface.

followups.because: because the Bypass_Duplicate_Rule_Lead Custom Permission must be granted via a scoped permission set assigned only to integration users, not added to a Profile.
because the next bulk Lead load against this org must verify the loader user holds the bypass permission and that the source data does not collide with existing Leads.
